title: US naval blockade of Iranian ports diverts 44 commercial vessels as Strait of Hormuz tensions escalate
summary: CENTCOM reported that 44 commercial vessels have turned around or returned to port under the ongoing US blockade of Iranian ports, as Iran simultaneously closes the Strait of Hormuz from the Arabian Sea side, creating dueling maritime blockades with major implications for global energy markets.
contents:
## Dueling Blockades in Strait of Hormuz Disrupt Global Shipping

### US Blockade Operations
The US Central Command (CENTCOM) confirmed that its active blockade of Iranian ports has now diverted 44 commercial vessels, directing them to turn around or return to port. The blockade represents one of the most significant maritime interdiction operations in recent decades.

### Iranian Counter-Blockade
Iranian Navy Rear Admiral Shahram Irani announced that Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz from the Arabian Sea, creating a dueling blockade scenario. The admiral warned of "swift and decisive action" if hostilities resume, underscoring the precarious nature of the maritime standoff.

### Economic Consequences
- Oil prices fell 0.4 percent following news of ongoing negotiations
- Global shipping routes through the Persian Gulf face significant disruption
- The Strait of Hormuz handles approximately 20% of global oil transit
- Insurance premiums for vessels transiting the region surged

### Diplomatic Efforts
Iran submitted a new peace proposal through Pakistani mediators. This second round of diplomatic outreach follows the Trump administration's rejection of a previous offer that would have delayed discussions on Iran's nuclear program. Iranian negotiators proposed reopening the Strait and negotiating nuclear restrictions separately, though President Trump demands a comprehensive nuclear deal.
